trajectory.description: Observation: Starting at iteration 0 with no workspace or history. Pretrained MetricGAN+ model available via speechbrain (task package, cached at /opt/pretrained_models). Analysis: MetricGAN+ is designed for speech enhancement and directly optimizes perceptual metrics like PESQ on VoiceBank+DEMAND-like data; strong baseline to validate pipeline, measure time, and get initial score before any training. Must test loading (network disabled so rely on local cache), usage for batch inference, and PESQ on val set. Decision: Write test_enhance.py to load model, enhance subset of val_noisy (50 samples for speed), compute avg PESQ vs val_clean, report timing. Copy pretrained to workspace for self-contained checkpoint. Follow up by creating minimal train.py (copies pretrained to ckpt) + full predict.py, then submit_eval for baseline.

trajectory.description: Observation: test_enhance.py failed at torchaudio.load due to missing torchcodec (default backend in recent torchaudio); model loading from local copy succeeded on CPU. Analysis: soundfile is pre-installed, loads mono WAVs directly to numpy (ideal for (T,) -> (1,T) tensor for enhance_batch); switch I/O, add GPU support, fix shape to (B,T), test on val subset for PESQ baseline (~2.8-3.2 expected for MetricGAN+ on this data). predict.py must be self-contained, offline-loadable from checkpoint dir, follow exact contract (no network, same filenames, 16kHz). Decision: update test_enhance.py, write full predict.py + minimal train.py (copies pretrained as ckpt), run test to validate+get val PESQ+time measurement, then submit_eval with this strong baseline before iterating.

trajectory.description: Observation: train.py failed due to missing logs/iter_1/ dir for metrics.jsonl (RAB_ITERATION env handling issue); test_enhance.py (soundfile-based, avoids torchaudio codec error) and predict.py exist but predict.py unverified for exact eval contract; MetricGAN+ checkpoint copied successfully in prior run, model loads offline. Analysis: MetricGAN+ is strong SE baseline (expected val PESQ ~2.8+); root cause of failure is missing os.makedirs for logs; predict.py must exactly follow arg parsing, load from --checkpoint dir, process test_files.txt from noisy/, save 16kHz WAVs with identical names, output JSON with absolute predictions_dir. Decision: rewrite train.py (create logs dir via env var, dummy metrics, prepare checkpoint), rewrite predict.py (full self-contained implementation matching contract, GPU support, soundfile I/O), run test_enhance.py for val PESQ+timing baseline, then submit_eval with metricgan_checkpoint to get official score and measure iter time before further optimization.

trajectory.description: Observation: MetricGAN+ baseline achieved strong test PESQ=3.134 (val~2.86). predict.py and test_enhance.py are functional with offline loading and correct contract. Analysis: Pretrained model is task-specific; further gains possible via post-processing (loudness normalization to -23 LUFS using pyloudnorm, common in SE to stabilize levels for PESQ). Training new model (e.g. ConvTasNet) from scratch would likely underperform initially and require many iters/debug. Decision: Update test_enhance.py and predict.py to add pyloudnorm normalization after enhancement (handle clipping), re-run test to measure val PESQ improvement, then submit_eval on same checkpoint with updated predict.py for potential new best.

trajectory.description: Observation: MetricGAN+ + loudness norm (-23 LUFS) achieved test PESQ=3.136 (val=2.862 on 100 samples), slight gains in cbak/covl. Warning on CUDA device parsing, test_enhance uses outdated speechbrain.pretrained import. Analysis: PESQ highly sensitive to signal level; sweeping loudness target on val set can find optimal (e.g. -20 to -28 range often best). Refactoring to function avoids dup code, fixes device, adds sweep over 30 targets on 30 samples (~70s compute, acceptable). Decision: update test_enhance.py for sweep + best target logging, run it to identify optimal loudness, then in next iter hardcode best into predict.py and submit_eval for potential new best.

Decision: Rewrite test_enhance.py cleanly (load best_target, test 4 strategies on 100 val samples: fixed, matching, iterative-fixed, iterative-matching), measure PESQ+time. trajectory.description: Observation: Current best PESQ=3.137 (fixed -25 LUFS norm on MetricGAN+; val PESQ flat at 2.8620 across fixed/mean_clean/match_noisy strategies on 100 samples, iterative worse). No training performed yet despite 12 iters; train.py exists but likely dummy (from early history). test_enhance.py and predict.py are mature, using speechbrain.inference.SpectralMaskEnhancement + pyloudnorm (offline from metricgan_checkpoint). Full val confirms mean clean LUFS~-24.16. Analysis: Post-processing saturated (flat PESQ curve); further gains require model improvement. ConvTasNet (asteroid) is pre-installed, SE-specific, easier to train/supervise with auraloss.SISDRLoss than fine-tuning MetricGAN+ (complex adversarial). Plan to edit train.py incrementally: add ConvTasNet training on chunked pairs (4s@16kHz), SI-SDR loss, 2-3 epochs on full data for baseline+time measurement. Keep MetricGAN fallback in predict.py. First inspect current train.py structure to ensure incremental edit (per guidelines). Decision: Use command to cat key files + ls, then next iter implement training + short run + submit_eval to measure iter time (~few min expected) before scaling epochs.

Asteroid.ConvTasNet (~8M params) + auraloss.SISDRLoss is lightweight, pre-installed, and directly applicable; chunked dataset enables fast iterations. Updated predict.py will load ConvTasNet from .pt checkpoint (keep loudness norm for consistency). 3 epochs on subsampled chunks + small val for initial time measurement/baseline (expect initial val PESQ ~1.5-2.2, will improve in future iters). Decision: Rewrite train.py (ConvTasNet training loop, dataset, logging to metrics.jsonl, save best.pt) and predict.py (load ConvTasNet + loudness from best_loudness.txt); execute training then submit_eval immediately for official score + per-iter time measurement before scaling epochs.

trajectory.description: Observation: ConvTasNet (1.2M params) after 3 epochs reaches train loss -10.86 but only val PESQ ~1.51 (test PESQ 1.836); far below MetricGAN+ best of 3.137 (with -25 LUFS norm). Val PESQ flat across post-processing strategies; train.py exists but produces poor enhancement (worse than no processing). test_enhance.py confirms best_target=-25.0 and baseline~2.862 on val. Last command ran training+val quickly (~13s/epoch). metrics.jsonl and best.pt saved. Analysis: Root cause is likely flawed training setup (e.g. incorrect target in loss, bad chunking, no proper normalization, model not learning clean speech recovery, or PESQ eval on mismatched lengths). MetricGAN+ is task-optimized for PESQ so hard to beat quickly; from-scratch model needs diagnosis + fixes (proper Dataset with paired chunks, auraloss.SISDRLoss+STFTLoss, AdamW scheduler, GPU batching, longer training). Reverting to MetricGAN in predict.py allows submitting strong baseline immediately while fixing training in parallel. Decision: Inspect current train.py + logs to diagnose, then incrementally edit train.py for correct SE setup (chunked paired data, combined losses, more epochs=20, log metrics), edit predict.py back to MetricGAN+ (from test_enhance logic, offline load from metricgan_checkpoint dir, -25 LUFS, /tmp/enhanced) for contract compliance, run test_enhance.py to validate, then submit_eval with metricgan_checkpoint to restore best score + measure time before scaling training.

trajectory.description: Observation: ConvTasNet (1.2M params) after 3 epochs reaches train loss -10.86 but only val PESQ ~1.51 (test 1.836) due to bugs (missing channel dim in inputs to model/loss, only 1000 chunks/epoch, short training). trajectory.description: Observation: ConvTasNet training crashes on ValueError (too many values to unpack) due to incorrect tensor shape in val loop: unsqueeze(0).unsqueeze(0) on already-batched (1,T) makes (1,1,1,T)=4D instead of (1,1,T). MetricGAN+ + loudness=-25 remains best (test PESQ=3.137, val=2.862 flat across strategies). test_enhance.py ran successfully, confirming setup. Analysis: Root cause is val data handling (DataLoader adds batch dim); fixing to .unsqueeze(1) + proper est.squeeze() will allow training. MetricGAN+ is task-specific/strong; beating it requires well-tuned ConvTasNet (more epochs, fixed loss, better chunks) but low-risk to restore best score now while enabling future training. Decision: Provide corrected full train.py (fixed shapes, best-model saving by val PESQ, metrics logging per epoch, 10 epochs for measurement), keep predict.py on MetricGAN+, run test+train (short), then submit_eval on metricgan_checkpoint to restore/confirm best PESQ before longer training runs.

ConvTasNet after 10 epochs reaches val PESQ~2.02 (train loss -14.2) but test_enhance.py crashed on torch.load of best.pt due to numpy.core.multiarray.scalar not allowed in weights_only=True (default in PyTorch 2.6); checkpoint likely contains optimizer/scheduler state with numpy scalars. train.py is functional but incomplete in view (full code ran previously). Analysis: MetricGAN+ (task-specific, PESQ-optimized) remains superior; ConvTasNet undertrained (only slight improvement over noisy baseline ~1.9). Root cause of load error is safe unpickling - fix via add_safe_globals. Hybrid (ConvTasNet pre-filter + MetricGAN) or longer ConvTasNet training (lower LR=5e-4, more epochs=30, better loss balance, full val=100) could help but risks time. Decision: Rewrite test_enhance.py (add safe_globals for numpy scalar, load both models, eval ConvTasNet w/ loudness norm on 100 val samples for fair comparison, test hybrid if time allows). Run it to diagnose ConvTasNet val PESQ w/ postproc. If >2.86 switch predict.py to ConvTasNet+norm or hybrid and submit_eval; else restore MetricGAN submit and plan longer ConvTasNet training in next iters (scale based on ~200s/iter).

trajectory.description: Observation: Best test PESQ stuck at 3.137 (MetricGAN+ w/ fixed -25 LUFS norm; val PESQ flat at 2.862 across all post-proc/hybrids). ConvTasNet (~1-5M params after capacity increases) plateaus ~2.02 val PESQ even after 30 epochs (train loss improves to ~-14 but insufficient recovery). test_enhance.py confirms MG superiority; train.py val loop incomplete/buggy in places; no model beats baseline yet. predict.py/test_enhance.py are mature for offline MetricGAN load + norm + /tmp/enhanced. Analysis: MetricGAN+ is task-specific (pretrained directly on VoiceBank+DEMAND to optimize PESQ), explaining why generic from-scratch ConvTasNet struggles in limited epochs/iterations. Post-processing and hybrids saturated (flat curves). With 3.3h/6 iters left, risky to commit more training runs that may not surpass 3.137 before budget exhaustion (each full train+test_enhance ~3-5min). Decision: Finalize with strongest config - write clean self-contained predict.py (uses speechbrain.inference.SpectralMaskEnhancement from metricgan_checkpoint, -25 LUFS via pyloudnorm, saves 16kHz mono WAVs to /tmp/enhanced per exact eval contract, handles args). Run test_enhance.py for confirmation, then submit_eval on metricgan_checkpoint to lock best score. No more ConvTasNet/HDemucs attempts to conserve budget.
